title: Lace canonical error code registry
description: Stable error/warning codes used by every Lace validator. Cross-implementation conformance depends on every validator emitting the same code for the same condition. The conformance vector schema (conformance-vector.schema.json) constrains expected error codes to this enum.

codes.description: Source text does not conform to the grammar.
Script contains zero HTTP calls.
A call has zero chain methods.
Chain methods appear in wrong order. Required: .expect → .check → .assert → .store → .wait.
A chain method appears more than once on the same call.
.expect() or .check() present with zero scopes.
.assert() present with zero conditions.
.store() present with zero entries.
`this` referenced outside chain methods or in a different call's chain.
`prev.*` referenced but no --prev-results provided.
Function call to a name not in the helper set (json/form/schema) outside an extension-permitted context.
.assert() expression failed to parse.
$var reference does not appear in the variable registry.
Same $$var assigned more than once across the script.
schema($var) argument is not a declared variable.
redirects.max exceeds context system maximum.
timeout.ms exceeds context system maximum.
timeout.retries set without timeout.action: "retry".
timeout.action value is not one of "fail", "warn", "retry".
clearCookies present but cookieJar is not a selective_clear variant.
cookieJar = "named:" with empty name.
cookieJar value does not match a recognised pattern.
op value is not one of: lt, lte, eq, neq, gte, gt.
maxBody size string does not match /\d+(k|kb|m|mb|g|gb)?/i.
Extension-registered field used but the registering extension is not active.
Script contains more than 10 calls.
Extension rule registers for an unknown hook name.
Extension rule emits to a target not declared in [result] or to a forbidden core path.
Extension emit to result.run_vars uses a key not prefixed with the extension name.
Helper function called with wrong argument type (e.g. json(string) instead of json(objectLit), schema(literal) instead of schema($var)).
